name: calendar
description: A full event calendar (month/week/day) rendered server-first (ADR-0012, R1 RESOLVED). The grid, the events placed in its cells, the view, the anchor date and the filter all live in typed Java; the server renders the grid as HTML and the client morphs each re-render. The wire optimization toolkit absorbs round-trip latency: l:init/skeleton for first paint, l:loading feedback, l:model.debounce filter, l:click navigation. The one irreducible client bit -- event drag onto a day cell -- is a tiny CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer that fires the moveEvent action on drop (no Lit, no @event-calendar). Retires the <lv-calendar> Lit island and gest's ht-calendar escape-hatch. WAI-ARIA APG grid pattern.
docs: Register CalendarComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Seed `allEvents` from your repository keyed by the visible range (rangeStart()..rangeEnd()) in the constructor / a @LievitRender hook -- the seed list is example data. Render it via the wire mount / @template.lievit.calendar(...); the cells + events are OWNED template markup (NOT a slot). Call enhanceCalendars(runtime) once from main.ts after startLievit() so dragging an event chip onto a day cell moves it server-side; add server-side authz in moveEvent(). The l:init skeleton makes first paint immediate; the l:model.debounce filter and l:click navigation re-render server-side. The @LievitComponent(template="lievit/calendar") name + the dev.lievit.wire package are yours to change once copied.
